summary:
The discovery of insulin’s structure and its interspecies variations were elucidated by Frederick Sanger through detailed sequencing studies; this led to his Nobel Prize awarding. \boxed{Frederick Sanger}

The summary concisely captures that it was Frederick Sanger who identified both the structural details and interspecies distinctions in insulin while highlighting why he deserved a Nobel Prize recognition for such findings.
